interactions.summary: Jiang answers the civil-war question by saying a U.S. civil war is part of the eschatology and is being manufactured through provocative ICE media operations, preconditioning films, protest conflict, and targeted shootings/arrests.
The host says Jiang would have been dismissed as conspiratorial in the 1990s and asks how young people will have to get used to once-conspiratorial material becoming mainstream.
Jiang says the key is attention, but young people are distracted, depressed, medicated, and educated into compliance; SSRIs and schooling produce numbness and compliancy.
The host asks about Jiang's work in education and what contemporary education should be for China and humanity.

speaker_notes.note: Two performed host questions: conspiracy/mainstream adaptation and education's future.
Single-word reaction has unclear diarization; likely host.

claims.claim: Jiang says American civil war is being manufactured through provocations around ICE, social-media content, emotional film narratives, and protest conflict.
Jiang says the next generation's primary problem is inability to pay attention because social media, depression, medication, and schools produce distraction and compliance.
Jiang says contemporary schooling teaches compliancy, which he treats as the core desired behavior of the control order.

signature_moments.moment: The young are not merely distracted; they are being trained into numb compliance.
signature_moments.source_phrase: They want a numb and indifferent population
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It connects education, medication, attention, and political obedience.

signature_moments.moment: A movie can pre-load the emotional script for a coming domestic conflict.
signature_moments.source_phrase: It almost makes you want to get involved in this war
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It shows Jiang's media-to-civil-war causal model.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The obedient, numb condition Jiang says schools and medication are cultivating.
The basic human capacity Jiang says the young generation is losing and the control system wants to capture.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang locates the ICE operation in Minnesota in early January and treats it as a current civil-war manufacturing example.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several film/person names are ASR-unclear; preserve the causal pattern rather than exact names unless verified.
